---
title: "About Me"
image: profile.png  # Add a photo of yourself to your project folder!
about:
  template: jolla    # 'jolla' puts the image in a circle at the top
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/yourhandle
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/yourname
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/yourusername
    - icon: google-scholar
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/
---

## Biography

I am Heba Labib; currently a Physics PhD student at North Carolina State University. I work at the intersection of theoretical condensed matter physics and quantum computing. My research focuses on quantum simulations of many-body systems as well as developing hardware-efficient quantum algorithms. I also work on developing theoretical frameworks for photo-emission spectroscopy and its applications in understanding the electronic properties of materials. I learn best by writing my own notes and explaining things to myself in writing; and thus, the reason I start this journal. In this blog, I will share my research progress, insights, and thoughts on various topics in physics and quantum computing.

## Research Interests

* **Topic 1:** Quantum Many-Body Systems and Quantum Simulations
* **Topic 2:** Photo-emission Spectroscopy and Electronic Properties of Materials
* **Topic 3:** Non-equilibrium Condensed Matter Dynamics 
* **Topic 4:** Quantum Algorithms and Quantum Computing

## Education

** North Carolina State University ** | Raleigh, NC  
PhD in [Physics] | Aug 2021 - Present
